data.title: U.S. crude stockpiles rose 2.3M barrels last week, API says
data.description:
The American Petroleum Institute reportedly shows a surprise build of 2.3M barrels of oil in U.S. commercial stockpiles for the week ending March 20, after increasing by 6.6M barrels in the previous week.

Gasoline inventories reportedly show a build of 500K barrels for the week, distillate inventories reportedly show a build of 1.4M barrels, and inventories at the Cushing storage hub reportedly show a build of 4M barrels.

The Energy Information Administration is scheduled to release its weekly U.S. petroleum supply report on Wednesday; the average of analysts surveyed by The Wall Street Journal forecast domestic commercial crude stocks would decline by 200K barrels, gasoline inventories were expected to drop by 2.4M barrels, and distillate inventories were expected to decline by 1.8M barrels.

WTI front-month April crude oil (CL1:COM) recently traded at $89.06/bbl, down from Tuesday's $92.35/bbl settlement price.

ETF: (USO)

data.title: Forgent Power Solutions launches public offering
data.description:
• Forgent Power Solutions (FPS) on Tuesday announced a public offering of its Class A common stock.
  • The offer consists of 20.68M shares of Class A common stock being offered by parent entities of the Company controlled by Neos Partners and 9.31M shares of Class A common stock being offered by Forgent.
  • In addition, the selling stockholders and the company intend to grant the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 3.10M shares and 1.39M shares, respectively, of Class A common stock at the public offering price, less underwriting discounts and commissions.
  • Forgent will not receive any proceeds from the sale of shares by the selling stockholders, and the net proceeds Forgent receives from the sale of its shares will be used to redeem interests in an operating subsidiary held by certain existing equity owners controlled by Neos Partners, LP. The operating subsidiary will bear or reimburse the company for all the expenses of the offering.
  • FPS -7.02% after hours to $33.27.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Tajiri Resources plans up to C$1.5M private placement
data.description:
• Tajiri Resources (TAJ:CA) announced a non-brokered private placement to raise up to C$1.5M.
  • The offering includes up to 8.33M units priced at C$0.18 per unit.
  • Each unit consists of one common share and half a warrant.
  • Each full warrant allows purchase of one share at C$0.40 within 18 months.
  • Proceeds will fund exploration, development, acquisitions, and working capital.

data.title: Grifols considers IPO of part of its US biopharma business
data.description:
• Spanish pharma Grifols, S.A. (GRFS) said that its board has decided to evaluate a potential US IPO of its shares of its US biopharma business.
  • The company said it was making the move as part of its "self-sufficiency program."
  • Grifols noted there is no guarantee an IPO will go through and would need to go through regulatory and legal requirements, internal approvals, and favorable market conditions.

data.title: Meta found guilty by N.M. jury in child safety trial; receives $375M penalty
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) was found guilty of violating state consumer protection laws by a New Mexico jury following a seven-week trial, and the owner of Facebook and Instagram will have to pay a $375M penalty.

Although the trial lasted nearly two months, it only took the jury one day to determine that Meta had violated sections of New Mexico's Unfair Practices Act. The state alleged the company engaged in “unconscionable” trade practices and willfully ignored evidence that its algorithms were exposing children to harmful and sensational content.

The prosecutors also alleged that the company failed to adequately enforce its minimum user age of 13, choosing profits over safety.

"The safety issues that you've heard about in this case weren't mistakes," said state prosecutor Linda Singer during closing arguments on Monday. "They were a product of a corporate philosophy that chose growth and engagement over children's safety. And young people in this state and around the country have borne the cost."

data.title: Gold's rise not over, just paused during Middle East war, BMO says
data.description:
Gold and silver prices could continue to trend lower from shifting economic conditions caused by the Middle East war, but the market's bullish momentum has only paused, not reversed, BMO analysts said in a note Tuesday.

"The Iran conflict does not take away from the structural thesis for metals and mining, but adds weight to it. It's just a question of when the market gains enough confidence that the conflict has reached a resolution before adding risk back on," BMO wrote.

The bank raised its outlook for gold prices to average $4,800/oz in Q3, up 7% from its previous forecast, and $4,900/oz in Q4, up 9% from its prior estimate, with the full-year average up to $4,846/oz from its earlier estimate of $4,550/oz.

BMO is bullish on gold through 2027, with prices expected to remain consistently above $5K and average $5,125 for the year, 26% higher than its previous estimate.

The bank also likes silver but thinks this year could represent the high-water mark for the metal, forecasting prices to average $74.50/oz in 2026 before slipping to average $64.20/oz next year.

"The long-term bull thesis for gold remains intact in our view—diversification, debasement, de-dollarization—but we may have to wait for the conflict to resolve for sellers to turn into buyers again," BMO wrote.

Gold and silver closed little changed Tuesday, with front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for March delivery down 0.1% to $4,399.30/oz, its fifth straight losing session, while Comex March silver (XAGUSD:CUR) was up 0.3% to $69.274/oz, snapping a nine-session losing streak that saw the front-month contract shedding more than 22% during the period.

data.title: Wesdome permanents interim COO
data.description:
• Wesdome Gold Mines (WDO:CA) appointed Tyler Mitchelson as chief operating officer.
  • Mitchelson transitions from interim COO, a role he has held since January 2026.

data.title: Stack Capital targets $15M in financing through private placement
data.description:
• Stack Capital Group (STCK:CA) announced a private placement targeting gross proceeds of up to $15 million.
  • The offering consists of 466,666 units under the LIFE Offering and 333,334 units under a Concurrent Private Placement, each unit including one common share and one-quarter warrant.
  • Management plans to subscribe for at least $1 million of units.
  • Proceeds will be used for investments, general corporate purposes, and working capital.
  • Closing is expected around March 31, 2026, subject to TSX approval and other conditions.
  • STCK:CA closed -2.10% at $19.1.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: U.S. reportedly has a plan to end the Iran war, according to NY Times
data.description:
The U.S. has reportedly sent Iran a 15-point plan to end the war in the Middle East, delivered through Pakistani intermediaries, according to two officials briefed on the diplomacy, the New York Times said.

The proposal reflects the Trump administration’s eagerness to find an off-ramp from the conflict, now in its fourth week, as it grapples with the war’s economic fallout.

The plan is said to address Iran’s ballistic missile and nuclear programs, as well as maritime routes through the Strait of Hormuz, where Iran has effectively blocked most Western ships, cutting global oil and natural gas supplies and sending prices soaring.

It remains unclear whether Israel, which has been bombing Iran alongside the U.S., is on board with the proposal.

White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt acknowledged diplomacy was underway but emphasized that military operations continue.

“As President Trump and his negotiators explore this newfound possibility of diplomacy, Operation Epic Fury continues unabated to achieve the military objectives laid out by the Commander in Chief and the Pentagon,” she said in a statement.

Pakistan’s army chief, Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir, has emerged as the key interlocutor between Washington and Tehran due to his believed close ties to Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps.

Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif of Pakistan wrote on social media that “subject to concurrence by the U.S. and Iran, Pakistan stands ready and honored to be the host to facilitate meaningful and conclusive talks for a comprehensive settlement of the ongoing conflict.”
